full_description: there are three RGB blocks on the table with randomized colors, pick the red block and move it to the center, then return the arm to origin
schema: {A} notifies the red block, {a} notifies the arm used to manipulate the red block
preference: num of words should not exceed 16. Degree of detail avg 7
seen: Use {a} to move {A} to the center and return to origin.
Pick {A} with {a}, place it at center, then reset {a}.
{a} grabs {A}, centers it, and goes back to origin.
Move {A} to center using {a}, then return {a} home.
Lift {A} by {a}, place at center, and reset {a}.
Use {a} to center {A}, then bring {a} to origin.
{a} picks {A}, sets it in center, then returns.
Place {A} at center with {a}, then move {a} to origin.
Relocate {A} by {a} to center and reset {a}.
{a} centers {A} and then returns to initial pose.
unseen: Transfer {A} to center via {a}, then send {a} to origin.
Bring {A} to center with {a} and reset {a}.
Set {A} at center by {a}, then recover {a} to origin.
Carry {A} with {a} to center and move {a} back.
Use {a} to place {A} at center, then reset {a} pose.
Pick-and-place {A} to center with {a}, then return {a}.
Center {A} through {a}, and move {a} to initial position.
Move {A} to center and have {a} go back to origin.
